meta.title: What's happening
meta.note: Curated cross-domain event log — the developments moving Mexico, dated and linked to a first-party or record source. Ranked by importance, then recency. Hand-curated for now; the auto-pipeline (DOF, Banxico, SESNSP, USTR) is the next layer.

events.title: UIF bloquea más de 120 mdp ligados al ‘huachicol’ y pide a bancos reforzar vigilancia contra el lavado de dinero
events.why: Como parte de la estrategia para reducir el ingreso de recursos derivados del robo de combustibles, la UIF y la CNBV emitieron una guía. La publicación UIF bloquea más de 120 mdp l

events.title: BNP Paribas prevé tres incrementos a la tasa de interés de Banxico para el 2027
events.why: Para este año, se espera que la tasa de interés de Banxico se mantenga sin movimientos en el 6.5%, nivel definido en junio pasado. La publicación BNP Paribas prevé tres incrementos

events.title: Vinci Airports, accionista de OMA, apuesta por México pese a desaceleración del tráfico aéreo
events.why: El CEO de Vinci Airports indicó que espera un crecimiento de tráfico más lento que en años anteriores por el alza en los precios de combustible. La publicación Vinci Airports, acci

events.title: Economía y PNUD alían esfuerzos para impulsar una política industrial con enfoque de desarrollo humano
events.why: Secretaría de Economía y PNUD acuerdan impulsar una política industrial con innovación, inclusión, digitalización y sostenibilidad en México.

events.title: IA y redes sociales, en la mira de Morena para el próximo periodo legislativo
events.why: Uno de los temas centrales que los legisladores buscarán debatir en las próximas semanas será la regulación de IA y de las redes sociales. La publicación IA y redes sociales, en la

events.title: Banobras prepara bolsa de 80 mil mdp para impulsar proyectos de energía renovable en México
events.why: Banobras y la Secretaría de Hacienda analizan un esquema de financiamiento de hasta 80 mil millones de pesos para impulsar proyectos de energía renovable.

events.title: Nu se convierte en el banco 54 en México tras alcanzar la rentabilidad y con 15 millones de clientes
events.why: La compañía de origen brasileño se convertirá en el banco número 54 operando en México, con ganancias que suman los 240 mdp. La publicación Nu se convierte en el banco 54 en México

events.title: Mexico to file U.S. criminal complaints over citizens' deaths in immigration enforcement
events.why: The sharpest escalation yet in the confrontation with Washington over the deportation crackdown — Mexico will pursue complaints against those responsible for 14 deaths in ICE custody and civil suits against private detention operators.

events.title: June inflation cools to 3.37% — lowest since December 2020
events.why: A third straight monthly decline, back inside Banxico's 3%±1% band. This is the print that underwrites the pause and keeps real rates high, supporting the peso.

events.title: May U.S. goods deficit with Mexico widens to $20.1B
events.why: The bilateral deficit is the administration's stated reason for not renewing USMCA. This is the freshest print, and it widened sharply from April's $14.8B.

events.title: Equifax to buy Círculo de Crédito, Mexico's #2 credit bureau, for $750M
events.why: The largest clean cross-border deal of the window — a bet on Mexican credit and fintech in a country where ~44% of adults are unbanked.

events.title: U.S. declines to renew USMCA, opening a 10-year annual-review cycle
events.why: The pact stays in force but is now renegotiated every year through 2036. It is the biggest overhang on the peso, on nearshoring investment, and on autos — the whole $1.8T relationship is an open question again.

events.title: May remittances rebound to a 2026-high $5.6B
events.why: A fourth straight month of annual growth, reversing the early-2026 scare — despite the new 1% U.S. remittance tax and tighter migration policy. Remittances are ~3.5% of GDP.

events.title: Banxico holds the policy rate at 6.50% — first unanimous vote, easing cycle over
events.why: The central monetary pivot: after two years of cuts, the board signals a prolonged pause, diverging from a Fed the market expects to ease. Minutes published July 9 reinforced it.

events.title: Sheinbaum confirms ~7 cabinet exits ahead of the 2027 elections
events.why: The pre-2027 realignment: Morena figures moving from government into party and campaign roles. The first read on how the 2027 midterm board is being set.

events.title: April economic activity (IGAE) rebounds +2.3% — strongest in ~5 years
events.why: The first hard evidence the economy is climbing out of the Q1 contraction (GDP −0.8% q/q), led by construction.

events.title: Government reports homicides at multi-year lows — down ~46% since it took office
events.why: The security narrative the whole administration rests on. Includes the lowest single day in a decade (~June 17) and the Plan Michoacán results (July 3). Note: the formal SESNSP monthly report for June isn't out until ~July 20 — these are cabinet/daily figures.

events.title: Economía tallies $7.15B in new investment; Q1 FDI a record $23.6B
events.why: The nearshoring / 'Plan México' story is still intact even under USMCA uncertainty — manufacturing, logistics, energy, pharma, aerospace.

events.title: Court keeps the 10% Section 122 surcharge on non-USMCA Mexican goods in force — expires July 24
events.why: The live, legally-contested tariff actually hitting non-USMCA-compliant goods right now, with a near-term expiry cliff. USMCA-qualifying goods are exempt.

events.title: White House expands Section 232 metals tariffs (steel, aluminum, copper)
events.why: Mexico is the single largest supplier of U.S. copper imports (~22%), so the 50% metals regime is a direct, concrete cost on top of the USMCA fight.

events.title: Second judicial reform published in the DOF: judicial elections pushed to 2028, Supreme Court restructured
events.why: Postpones the pending federal and local judicial elections from 2027 to 2028 and reshapes the Court (rotating two-year presidency, two sections). A separate decree adds foreign interference as grounds to void an election. Secondary laws are moving through Congress.

events.title: Moody's cuts Mexico to Baa3, the last investment-grade rung
events.why: Chiefly the cost of propping up Pemex plus structural deficits — the credit story of the moment. Standing ratings: Moody's Baa3 stable, S&P BBB negative, Fitch BBB- stable.
